title: Implement a Least Frequently Used (LFU) Cache
statement:
Design a caching system with a Least Frequently Used (LFU) eviction strategy. The system should support the following endpoints:

### POST /api/cache

Create a new cache with a specified capacity. The request body should contain the capacity.

### POST /api/cache/:cacheId/put/:key/:value

Add or update an item in the cache. The request body should contain the key and value.

### GET /api/cache/:cacheId/get/:key

Retrieve an item from the cache. If the item is not found, return a 404 status code.

### GET /api/cache/:cacheId/stats

Retrieve statistics about the cache, including the number of items, the number of items that have been evicted, and a list of the most frequently accessed items.

### DELETE /api/cache/:cacheId

Delete the cache.

You must use the in-memory array provided in the starter code. You should not store the cache data in a database or use an external caching system.

#### Constraints

- The cache should use an LFU eviction strategy.
- The cache should support a maximum capacity.
- The cache should support GET, PUT, and DELETE operations.
- The cache should support retrieval of statistics.
- The cache should be case-sensitive.
- The cache should handle edge cases, such as trying to put an item into a cache that is at capacity.
constraints: Must use only the in-memory array provided in the starter code.
Cache should use an LFU eviction strategy.
